items.note:
[Backend] Workload sync should retry on failure
Context: the nightly capacity sync sometimes leaves a team with no numbers.
Impact: managers open the map to an empty state and lose trust in it.
Suggestion: retry the sync a few times before giving up, and show the last-good data meanwhile.
items.triageNote: Real defect that erodes trust in the map. Assigned to Wave 3 (Real Data Go-live) - sync reliability + last-good fallback belong to the data-go-live work.

items.note:
[Bug] Export button does nothing on the weekly report
Context: clicking Export gives no file and no error.
Impact: I can't share the week with my director.
Suggestion: it should download a PDF or show why it can't.
items.triageNote: A dead button is a real bug, not a nice-to-have. Moved from Testing to Assigned. The weekly report is an Executive-deck surface, so it belongs to Wave 1 (Executive Status Deck) to fix now.

items.note:
[Feature] Dark mode for the printed report
Context: the report is bright white when printed at night.
Impact: minor, cosmetic.
Suggestion: a dark theme for the report page.
items.triageNote: Cosmetic and low-value, kept at Review (not yet started). Assigned to Wave 1 (Executive Status Deck) because the printed report is a delivery-deck surface - that is the wave that would own it when a print-polish pass opens.

items.note:
[Feature] Shareable weekly delivery digest
Context: directors want to forward "what shipped this week" without opening the app.
Impact: turns the page into a broadcast tool; less status-meeting time.
Suggestion: a one-click export (image or link) of the current week: % delivered, what shipped, what needs a decision.
items.triageNote: High-value and squarely Wave 1 (Executive Status Deck owns broadcast/reporting). Assigned to Wave 1.

items.note:
[Feature] Burnout early-warning alerts
Context: overload is spotted too late, usually after someone is already stressed.
Impact: protects people and lets managers rebalance before it becomes a resignation risk.
Suggestion: flag a person/team when sustained load crosses a threshold, with a quiet nudge to the manager (track work, never surveil).
items.triageNote: Serves Wave 2 (Capacity Engine) directly and aligns with the human-first principle. Assigned to Wave 2.

items.note:
[Feature] Auto-post "what shipped this week" to Slack
Context: the delivery story lives in the app; the team lives in Slack.
Impact: keeps everyone aware with zero effort and drives people back to the page for detail.
Suggestion: a scheduled Slack message summarising the weeks shipped items + a link, posted every Friday.
items.triageNote: Clean fit for Wave 4 (Slack Integration). Assigned to Wave 4.

items.note:
[Enhancement] Trend arrow on each wave card
Context: a wave card shows todays state but not whether it is getting better or worse.
Impact: lets a director see momentum at a glance, not just a snapshot.
Suggestion: add a small up/down arrow vs last weeks progress on each wave card (we already store history[]).
items.triageNote: Low-effort, high-signal improvement to the delivery view itself. Assigned to Wave 1.

items.note:
[Enhancement] Group the timeline by wave
Context: the timeline is chronological; sometimes you want "everything in Wave 2".
Impact: faster to answer "how is this wave doing" without scanning dates.
Suggestion: an optional "group by wave" toggle alongside the Week/All navigator.
items.triageNote: SHIPPED as wave-focus (click a wave card to scope the timeline). Belongs to Wave 1. Closing the loop.

items.note:
[Frontend] Faster first paint on the Workload map
Context: the map can feel slow on first load with a full org.
Impact: better perceived performance; less waiting for the most-opened screen.
Suggestion: skeleton + progressive render so the shell appears instantly and data fills in.
items.triageNote: Valid polish, still at Review (not started). Assigned to Wave 2 (Capacity Engine) - the Workload map is the Capacity surface, so this perf work belongs to that wave.

items.note:
[New skill] Convert a feedback idea into a PR draft in one click
Context: today an accepted idea still has to be written up by hand to become work.
Impact: closes the loop from idea to shipped work; less manual transcription.
Suggestion: a button on a triaged item that opens a pre-filled PR/issue draft (blocked until the token-safe write proxy exists).
items.triageNote: Depends on the still-open write-proxy blocker, so kept at Review. Assigned to Wave 1 (Executive Status Deck) - it closes the delivery loop (idea to shipped work) which is Wave 1's remit.

items.note:
[Backend] Monday director digest email
Context: directors do not always remember to open the page.
Impact: brings the delivery story to them at the start of the week.
Suggestion: a scheduled Monday email with the same digest as the shareable one (reuses idea-1).
items.triageNote: Depends on idea-1 and an email transport we do not have yet, so kept at Review. Assigned to Wave 1 (Executive Status Deck) - same delivery-broadcast family as idea-1, which owns it.

items.note:
[Enhancement] Freshness + confidence badge on every derived number
Context: the page derives everything; a viewer cannot tell how fresh or solid a number is.
Impact: reinforces trust and the derive-never-assert principle - no number looks more certain than it is.
Suggestion: a small "as of <time>" / "based on N PRs" badge next to the headline figures.
items.triageNote: Cross-cutting design task, kept at Review for a design pass. Assigned to Wave 3 (Real Data Go-live) - freshness/confidence on derived numbers is exactly the trust layer that go-live needs, so that wave owns it.

items.note:
[Feature] One-click rebalance suggestion
Context: managers can SEE overload but still rebalance by gut; the tool stops at diagnosis.
Impact: closes the loop from insight to action - the difference between a dashboard and a decision tool (the product north star).
Suggestion: when a person is flagged overloaded, suggest the 2-3 best under-loaded peers to move a task to, with why. Manager confirms.
items.triageNote: The strategic anchor for Wave 2. Diagnosis without a recommended action is a report, not a COO. Highest impact/effort ratio in the wave.

items.note:
[Enhancement] Capacity forecast (next 2 weeks)
Context: load is shown as today only; managers plan the fortnight blind.
Impact: shifts capacity from reactive to proactive - prevent the overload before it happens.
Suggestion: project each person forward from assigned events/deadlines and show a simple green/amber/red per week.
items.triageNote: Second Wave-2 bet. Forecasting is where capacity tools create real planning value; medium effort, high strategic fit.

items.note:
[New skill] Data-quality gate before go-live
Context: switching from sample to real data risks shipping wrong numbers that destroy trust on day one.
Impact: protects the single most fragile moment - first impressions of real data decide adoption.
Suggestion: a pre-go-live checklist that validates completeness/freshness/outliers and blocks the cutover until green.
items.triageNote: The make-or-break for Wave 3. A bad first data load is unrecoverable trust damage. This is risk insurance for the whole go-live.

items.note:
[Backend] Last-good-data fallback everywhere
Context: if a live sync fails, screens can go blank - exactly what erodes trust (see sample-1).
Impact: graceful degradation keeps the tool credible under real-world data hiccups.
Suggestion: cache the last successful pull per view and show it with an "as of <time>" note when a refresh fails.
items.triageNote: Reinforces sample-1. A resilience pattern that pays off across every data-backed view; do it once, benefit everywhere.

items.note:
[Feature] Actionable Slack alerts (approve/assign inline)
Context: a Slack post that only links back to the app adds a click; the decision still happens elsewhere.
Impact: brings the DECISION into Slack - far higher response rate, the real point of the integration.
Suggestion: for a "needs you" item, post a Slack message with Approve / Assign-to-wave buttons that write back.
items.triageNote: Depends on the token-safe write proxy (same blocker as feedback Send), so kept at Review - honest, not inflated. Assigned to Wave 4 (Slack Integration), which clearly owns it once the blocker lands.

items.note:
[Enhancement] Decision log (why we did / dropped things)
Context: triage decisions live in scattered PRs; the WHY behind the roadmap is not visible in one place.
Impact: institutional memory - the thing that makes a COO trustworthy is a traceable reason for every call. Directly serves the Trust principle.
Suggestion: a simple, filterable log of every triaged item + its reason, readable on the delivery page.
items.triageNote: Cross-cutting but Wave-1 (executive visibility) owns it. High strategic value: traceability is a core product principle, and we already capture triageNote - surface it.

items.note:
[Enhancement] Role-based landing (COO sees delivery, manager sees their team)
Context: everyone lands on the same dashboard regardless of what they need first.
Impact: reduces time-to-value per role; a small change with broad daily payoff.
Suggestion: route each role to the view they most need on open, with a one-tap switch to the rest.
items.triageNote: Good UX leverage but not urgent, so kept at Review. Assigned to Wave 1 (Executive Status Deck) - role-based landing is an executive-visibility improvement, so that wave owns it.
